qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: antigravity module not in UNSAFE_IMPORTS blocklist enabling SSRF and phishing attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable code (UNSAFE_IMPORTS frozenset in fickling/fickle.py) and same root cause (incomplete blocklist missing dangerous stdlib modules). The advisory describes missing uuid, _osx_support, _aix_support; this finding identifies antigravity as another missing module. Same CWE-184 incomplete deny list, same mechanism: modules absent from UNSAFE_IMPORTS pass security screening.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: EXT1/EXT2/EXT4 opcodes cause analyzer crash and bypass security checks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: this finding describes an AttributeError crash when UnsafeImportsML.analyze() accesses node.module on ast.Import nodes (analysis.py:287), not the incomplete UNSAFE_IMPORTS blocklist in fickle.py. The advisory is about missing modules in UNSAFE_IMPORTS; this is about a parser crash in the analyzer.

selected_finding.title: antigravity module not in UNSAFE_IMPORTS blocklist enabling SSRF and phishing attacks

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The antigravity_module_bypass finding identifies the same vulnerable code and root cause as the advisory: the UNSAFE_IMPORTS frozenset in fickling/fickle.py is an incomplete blocklist. The advisory describes missing uuid, _osx_support, and _aix_support; the finding identifies antigravity as another missing dangerous stdlib module. The PoC demonstrates that a properly-formed pickle importing antigravity passes check_safety() with LIKELY_SAFE severity, confirming the incomplete blocklist allows dangerous modules to bypass security screening.
evidence: A properly-formed pickle (0x80048c0b616e7469677261766974798c04786b6364932e) importing antigravity passes check_safety() with Severity.LIKELY_SAFE
UNSAFE_IMPORTS contains 102 modules and does not include 'antigravity'
The advisory-specific modules (uuid, _osx_support, _aix_support) are blocked in the installed version (0.1.11), confirming the advisory fix
antigravity is a real Python stdlib module that executes webbrowser.open() on import, demonstrating code execution capability
webbrowser IS in UNSAFE_IMPORTS but antigravity (which wraps it) is NOT, showing the blocklist is still incomplete
Same vulnerable code location: UNSAFE_IMPORTS frozenset in fickling/fickle.py

poc_executions.command: python3 -c '... corrected exploit constructing valid antigravity import pickle ...'
poc_executions.observed_effect: A properly-formed pickle importing 'antigravity' (a stdlib module not in UNSAFE_IMPORTS) passes check_safety() with LIKELY_SAFE. The UNSAFE_IMPORTS frozenset has 102 modules and excludes antigravity. The antigravity module exists in Python stdlib and executes webbrowser.open() when imported. The advisory-specific modules (uuid, _osx_support, _aix_support) are blocked in the installed version, confirming the fix was applied for those modules while the blocklist remains incomplete for others.
